description: In June 2020, Ubisoft, a major video game publisher, faced a massive wave of public and internal allegations regarding systemic sexual harassment, bullying, and a toxic 'boys' club' culture. The scandal led to the resignation of top executives and sparked a global conversation about labor rights, unionization, and accountability in the gaming industry.

key_events.description: Initial reports emerge on Twitter accusing Ubisoft executives of rampant harassment.
Ubisoft CEO Yves Guillemot issues a public apology and begins structural leadership overhaul.
The 'ABetterUbisoft' movement gains traction, with employees demanding concrete changes and union support.
Ubisoft announces it will retain some accused leaders in different roles, sparking a massive backlash against the firm's perceived lack of progress.

calibration_notes: This scenario calibrates how quickly initial vague rumors coalesce into a strong, unified public consensus against corporate leadership when faced with credible whistleblower testimony.
